title: Applications of Cayley-Hamilton Theorem
description: A flowchart showing the core concept and practical applications of the Cayley-Hamilton Theorem in linear algebra.
relationships: Theorem states that substituting matrix A into its own characteristic polynomial yields the zero matrix
This relationship allows solving for A⁻¹ in terms of powers of A
Enables computation of higher powers A^k without direct multiplication
Facilitates evaluation of analytic functions of the matrix (e.g., e^A) using the polynomial identity
